## prompt-full-passage

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-enactment.md`, adapted
from the prior build's `adoption/sub-prompts/prompt-3-full-framework.md` and the
processed-manuscript conventions of `adoption/full-framework`. This build is
analogous to, and does not rewrite, the prior adoption framework.*

Write the full-framework into `law-physical-ai-trials/enactment/full-framework/` by
processing every bracketed `[DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS: ...]` left in
`enactment/draft-framework/`. Replace each bracket with finished, publication-grade
prose drawn from the exact files and directories it names. The result is the
complete second iteration: no scaffolding brackets remain, every section reads as a
senior policy-author wrote it, and the manuscript gains technical substance as it
proceeds, defining each term the first time it appears for a legislator or staffer
who has neither robotics nor Claude Code and Codex experience.

**Use the named sources effectively.** Ground the eight passage-question sections
in: the bill text in
`single-prompt-bill/auto-bill-02/final-bill/publication` (the findings of section 2,
the section 515D amendment and the ten conforming amendments of section 3, the
comparative print of section 4, the financial transparency and authorization of
section 5, and the cost-estimate, verification-economics, and financial-standard
appendices); the platform scope in
`physical-ai-oncology-trials/national-platform/new_paper/final_paper` (the
executive summary, the national MCP servers, the PSL and USL standards, the
financial analysis, the regulatory landscape, and the implementation strategy); the
bill and prior-work lineage in `enactment/references/author_works.bib` (entry
`Kawchak2026HR9510Bill`, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762, and the platform and
patient-journey works); and the legislative and standards references in
`enactment/references/passage_refs.bib`. Each passage claim is paired with a
credible, cited fact so the argument is defensible in a markup and on the floor.

**Carry the prompt content into the body.** Weave in the concrete passage levers
(the embodiment problem and the sequencing gap; the cost asymmetry; the demonstrated
and affordable feasibility record; the closest existing analogues and the consensus
standards basis; budget discipline under PAYGO and CutGo) where each is most
relevant, so the framework reads as a working passage playbook and not only an
argument. Keep the recurring questions ("Would I defend this vote to a constituent
who lost someone to a preventable error?", "Can I explain the score to the Budget
Committee?", "Is there daylight between my party and the other on this?") as motifs.

**Figures and tables.** Render the colored TikZ Mermaid figures from
`enactment/mermaid/` into the body in the strict palette (black, grayscales,
`#4B0082`, `#000080`, `#C0C0C0`), distributed across the sections, the introduction,
the framework, and the appendix, so the manuscript carries the 20 figures called for
in the schedule. **Set `\vspace{-0.75cm}` between every figure and its caption.**
Every table is set to `\textwidth` with `>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}`
columns whose widths are chosen for the amount of text each column carries, and is
referenced with `\autoref` rather than narrated row by row.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty`, `references.bib`, and `README.md`; one
commit per `sections/*.tex` file; plus `prompt-full-passage.md` and
`output-full-passage.md`. For the second-to-last commit fix all errors across all
files; for the last commit write the directory README and the
`full-framework-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle and refresh the pull request. The
manuscript must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX. Maintain the `#4B0082` template
visual theme. Commit and push in real time; do not hold commits. Do not stall, ask
questions, or go into plan mode.
